blockers: Plugin management lacks canonical keys, immutable revisions, complete provenance, compare-and-swap, idempotency, and rollback receipts.
Plugin inventory omits opted-in project plugins even though the runtime can load them.
MCP list is a redacted partial projection and cannot safely seed whole-map replacement or rollback.
MCP canonical configuration, credential presence, and live per-process connection status are split across different surfaces.
Native mutations do not accept expected state fingerprints or idempotency keys.
OAuth revoke and unified MCP removal/credential cleanup are unsupported.
recommendation: Keep Cabinet plugin and MCP mutations disabled. Upstream canonical versioned readbacks and expected-version/idempotent mutation receipts in Hermes, then bind the preview-only governed envelopes to those native contracts.
